content:
type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
spec: Add case_sensitive parameter to groupby filter. Signature change for both sync_do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive: bool = False) and async do_groupby (same new param, added after default, keeping default as previous positional/keyword param before it for backward compat). Behavior: build key = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None, default=default); use this key for both sorted() and itertools.groupby() so items with same normalized value land in one group. When case_sensitive is False, the displayed grouper value must preserve the ORIGINAL case of the first item encountered in that group (not the lowercased key) -- e.g. ['CA','NY','ca'] sorted/grouped by ignore_case should yield group grouper=='CA' (since 'CA' sorts/appears before 'ca' - Python sort is stable and 'CA' < 'ca' lowercased tie is broken by original stable order) containing both 'CA' and 'ca' items. Concretely: after grouping with the normalized key, replace each group's grouper with raw_expr(values[0]) where raw_expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) (no postprocess) -- i.e. re-derive the attribute from the first item in the resolved group list, still producing _GroupTuple(grouper, list(values)). When case_sensitive is True, behavior is unchanged from current implementation (grouper is exactly the attribute value, no lowercasing). Apply the same change mirrored in the async do_groupby using auto_to_list for the async iterable. Add a  docstring note similar to do_sort's versionchanged entries. Docstring param doc: ':param case_sensitive: Group strings with different casing as separate groups. Defaults to False, groups are case insensitive.' No changes needed to _GroupTuple or ignore_case (already exist at module level, reuse them).
note: case_sensitive default False for backward-incompatible but requested UX; if you want strict backward compat you could default True instead -- but task requests default False to match sort() and produce case-insensitive grouping by default

Behavior: build key = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None, default=default); use this key for both sorted() and itertools.groupby() so items with same normalized value land in one group. When case_sensitive is False, the displayed grouper value must preserve the ORIGINAL case of the first item encountered in that group (not the lowercased key) -- e.g. ['CA','NY','ca'] sorted/grouped by ignore_case should yield group grouper=='CA' (since 'CA' sorts/appears before 'ca' - Python sort is stable and 'CA' < 'ca' lowercased tie is broken by original stable order) containing both 'CA' and 'ca' items. Concretely: after grouping with the normalized key, replace each group's grouper with raw_expr(values[0]) where raw_expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) (no postprocess) -- i.e. re-derive the attribute from the first item in the resolved group list, still producing _GroupTuple(grouper, list(values)). When case_sensitive is True, behavior is unchanged from current implementation (grouper is exactly the attribute value, no lowercasing). Apply the same change mirrored in the async do_groupby using auto_to_list for the async iterable. Add a  docstring note similar to do_sort's versionchanged entries. Docstring param doc: ':param case_sensitive: Group strings with different casing as separate groups. Defaults to False, groups are case insensitive.' No changes needed to _GroupTuple or ignore_case (already exist at module level, reuse them).
